 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","title":"Integrate FrankenSQLite backend adapter for concurrent durable recorder writes","description":"## Context
User intent explicitly calls out evaluating/adapting `/dp/frankensqlite` (multi-writer WAL + self-healing ideas) for durable high-concurrency logging.

## Scope
Design and implement a FrankenSQLite-backed recorder adapter behind `RecorderStorage`, including concurrency, WAL settings, and corruption/recovery policy boundaries.

## Deliverables
- Adapter implementation mapping recorder event batches to FrankenSQLite schema
- Concurrency model (writer sharding, lock strategy, or queueing) documented and benchmarked
- WAL tuning + self-healing strategy notes (what is adopted vs deferred)
- Validation tests for correctness under concurrent writer stress

## Why this matters
This is the highest-upside durability/performance path and the main mechanism for learning from frankensqlite in production-adjacent conditions.

## Acceptance
- Adapter passes contract tests from storage abstraction
- Concurrency behavior is reproducible and does not violate ordering invariants
- Recovery behavior is documented with explicit operator expectations","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:45:14.796373-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:45:14.796373-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented and validated FrankenSQLite recorder storage adapter with contract + concurrent writer tests","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","frankensqlite","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.068191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.34223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.801271Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":39,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Validated FrankenSQLite recorder adapter implementation in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs (FrankenSqliteStorageConfig + FrankenSqliteRecorderStorage) including WAL/bootstrap, monotonic ordinals, idempotent batch commits, checkpoint monotonicity persistence, and concurrent writer coverage. Verification run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage -- --nocapture and CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core frankensqlite -- --nocapture (all relevant tests passed).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:45:00Z"},{"id":43,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"Implemented FrankenSQLite recorder backend adapter in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: added FrankenSqliteStorageConfig + FrankenSqliteRecorderStorage with WAL-mode schema/bootstrap, bounded in-flight admission, idempotent batch commits, monotonic ordinal offsets, checkpoint persistence/monotonicity, lag metrics, and health/flush integration through RecorderStorage. Added backend tests for monotonic offsets, idempotency, checkpoint persistence across reopen, and concurrent writer ordinal uniqueness. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets; CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-codex cargo test -p frankenterm-core frankensqlite -- --nocapture (4/4 new tests passed).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:43:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","title":"Implement crash-safe checkpointing and deterministic replay cursor semantics","description":"## Context
Indexer and replay systems require reliable continuation after process restarts/crashes. We need explicit checkpoints and idempotent resume semantics from storage.

## Scope
Implement write/checkpoint metadata and restart replay protocol so downstream consumers can resume from last committed offset without duplication or gaps.

## Deliverables
- Durable checkpoint format and atomic update protocol
- Resume/read APIs for indexers and replayers
- Duplicate suppression strategy where at-least-once replay is unavoidable
- Crash/restart integration tests

## Why this matters
Without deterministic replay/resume semantics, indexing quality and forensic trustworthiness degrade quickly.

## Acceptance
- Restart from crash resumes at deterministic point
- Replay APIs provide no silent gap behavior
- Duplicate handling strategy is explicit and tested","notes":"Claimed to complete replay/checkpoint semantics after wa-oegrb.3.3 closure; fixing partial recorder_storage replay API breakage and adding crash/resume coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:52:59.228272-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented crash-safe checkpoint resume cursor semantics with deterministic replay API and tests","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","reliability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.115436Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.393544Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.435384Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":40,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Implemented missing replay/checkpoint semantics in crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs: added ReplayCursor/ReplayBatch/ReplayResumePlan contract types, trait surface (plan_resume/replay_batch), append-log replay reader with deterministic ordinal cursoring and torn-tail-safe behavior, and crash/restart resume tests. Also wired health() to include pipeline metrics default to satisfy expanded health schema. Validation: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo check -p frankenterm-core --all-targets (passes); CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target_gentlesnow cargo test -p frankenterm-core recorder_storage -- --nocapture (8 recorder_storage tests passed, including new resume/replay tests).","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:52:55Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","title":"Define retention, partitioning, and archival lifecycle for append-only recorder data","description":"## Context
Full-fidelity recorder data can grow rapidly. Retention and partitioning choices must be first-class so we do not accumulate unbounded costs or operational risk.

## Scope
Define and implement retention windows, partition strategy (time/size), and archival/export hooks for append log segments.

## Deliverables
- Retention policy model (default + override scopes)
- Segment lifecycle logic (active, sealed, archived, purged)
- Tooling hooks for safe archival/export
- Tests proving retention operations preserve invariants and auditability

## Why this matters
Cost and operability determine whether this feature can remain always-on in real swarm usage.

## Acceptance
- Retention behavior is deterministic and observable
- Purge/archive operations do not corrupt offset continuity semantics
- Operators can reason about storage growth via documented policy","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.606628Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.162884Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.479191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.60657Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","title":"Add deep storage-pipeline telemetry, diagnostics, and SLO-oriented metrics","description":"## Context
The writer path is mission-critical; without deep telemetry we cannot tune or safely roll out. We need first-class metrics and tracing for throughput, lag, failures, and durability state.

## Scope
Instrument storage pipeline with metrics/events/logging suitable for SLOs and incident triage.

## Deliverables
- Metrics for append rate, batch size, queue depth, flush latency, error classes, replay lag
- Structured diagnostics for backend health and checkpoint progression
- Alert recommendations for critical degradation patterns
- Documentation mapping metrics to likely remediation actions

## Why this matters
Observability is necessary both for performance tuning and for proving recorder trustworthiness in incidents.

## Acceptance
- Critical failure/lag states are externally visible
- Metrics are stable enough for automated thresholds
- Documentation allows operators to diagnose common failure modes","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T18:09:21.64036-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","observability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.523344Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.569934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","title":"Add deep storage-pipeline telemetry, diagnostics, and SLO-oriented metrics","description":"## Context

## Acceptance

- Documentation allows operators to diagnose common failure modes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T18:35:03.575858-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T18:35:03.575858-05:00","close_reason":"36-test storage_telemetry module: latency histograms, throughput counters, EWMA rate, health tiers, SLO tracking, diagnostics","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","observability","track-storage"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.226861Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.523344Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.3.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:05:50.569934Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4","title":"Indexing track: Tantivy lexical indexing, incremental ingestion, rebuild tooling","description":"## Track objective
Design and implement lexical indexing over recorder logs using Tantivy with near-real-time incremental ingestion and robust rebuild/repair workflows.

## Why this track exists
The recorder only creates value if data is rapidly discoverable. Lexical search is the baseline reliability layer and must remain available regardless of semantic subsystem state.

## Deliverables
- Tantivy schema and analyzer strategy
- Incremental indexer pipeline from append-only log
- Query semantics for phrase, boolean, field filters, time windows
- Rebuild/checkpoint/repair tools

## Acceptance criteria
- Lexical search latency and correctness targets are documented and test-enforced.
- Index can be rebuilt from append-only logs without data ambiguity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:49.68422-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T17:49:49.68422-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children closed: schema design (4.1), incremental ingestion (4.2), commit/merge policy (4.3), reindex/backfill tooling (4.4), lexical query service (4.5), quality harness (4.6). Full lexical indexing track complete.","labels":["indexing","lexical","search","tantivy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.381913Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.755747Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","title":"Design versioned Tantivy schema for recorder text + metadata filters","description":"## Context
Lexical retrieval quality and index stability depend on getting schema decisions right early (field structure, analyzers, stored vs indexed fields, offset references, metadata filters).

## Scope
Design the canonical Tantivy schema for recorder events, including text fields, normalized metadata, and time/agent/pane filters.

## Deliverables
- Versioned Tantivy schema definition with migration notes
- Field-by-field rationale (why indexed/stored/tokenized)
- Analyzer/tokenizer strategy tuned for terminal text (commands, stack traces, mixed symbols)
- Golden tests for schema compatibility and queryability

## Why this matters
Schema drift or poor tokenization destroys search quality and makes reindexing expensive.

## Acceptance
- Schema supports required filters and retrieval use-cases
- Analyzer choices are justified with examples
- Versioning strategy is documented for future migrations","notes":"Delivered Tantivy lexical schema contract in docs/flight-recorder/tantivy-schema-v1.md, including field-by-field schema, tokenizer strategy for terminal text/symbols, migration/rebuild semantics, and golden compatibility/queryability test plan. Linked contract from ADR and cross-project extraction matrix for downstream implementation beads.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:29:53.243622-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:27:34.625042-05:00","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.46742Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.826595Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.855245Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.883639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","title":"Implement incremental Tantivy ingestion from append-log checkpoints","description":"## Context
Recorder writes are append-only; indexer must consume offsets incrementally with deterministic resume behavior and bounded lag.

## Scope
Implement incremental index ingestion from recorder checkpoints, including idempotent retries and commit cadence controls.

## Deliverables
- Offset-driven incremental indexer pipeline
- Checkpoint coordination with storage replay cursors
- Commit/flush policy for freshness vs throughput tradeoffs
- Integration tests for restart/resume without data loss

## Why this matters
Incremental indexing is the bridge between high-throughput logging and usable near-real-time search.

## Acceptance
- Indexer resumes deterministically after restarts
- No silent drops/duplication under normal retry paths
- Lag is measurable and bounded under target load","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented incremental Tantivy ingestion pipeline: AppendLogReader (binary log scanning, seek-to-ordinal, torn-tail safe), IndexDocumentFields (all 24 schema fields), map_event_to_document for all 4 event types with redaction handling, IndexWriter trait for pluggable backends, IncrementalIndexer with checkpoint-coordinated batching/resume/dedup, IndexerLagMonitor. 40 tests covering cold start, resume from checkpoint, multi-pane, mixed types, schema mismatch skip, commit failure propagation, lag monitoring.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.652992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.911617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
